claim: SWE-bench can be described as a benchmark that asks language models to edit real codebases in response to GitHub issue descriptions, rather than only solve isolated coding exercises.
support_quality: primary arXiv abstract plus local capture
notes: Allowed: repository-issue framing, real GitHub issues, codebase editing, multi-file complexity. Blocked: live leaderboard or current best-model claims.

claim: LiveCodeBench can be described as a contamination-focused and broader code-evaluation benchmark that continuously collects new contest problems and evaluates capabilities beyond plain code generation.
support_quality: primary arXiv abstract plus local capture
notes: Allowed: contamination-free framing, new problems over time, LeetCode/AtCoder/CodeForces source platforms, self-repair/execution/test-output prediction. Blocked: current leaderboard or provider superiority.

claim: Claude Code can be framed as a terminal/repository agent in which context management, tool permissions, file edits, command execution, tests, and human review form the work loop.
notes: Allowed: structural workflow synthesis. Blocked: exact settings/defaults beyond captured security wording, productivity claims, adoption claims, and enterprise outcomes.

claim: Exact Claude 4 SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-bench, or best-coding-model claims require benchmark version, harness/scaffold, tool permissions, sample budget, date, and source snapshot before charting.
notes: The chapter intentionally uses Anthropic benchmark language only as vendor-attributed positioning and does not reproduce numeric comparative claims.

claim: Anthropic security docs support saying Claude Code is read-only by default and requests permission for additional actions such as edits, tests, and command execution, with user responsibility for reviewing proposed code and commands.
notes: Allowed as captured-page product behavior; future final draft should recheck exact current docs and permissions/settings pages before quoting defaults.
